data.title: Bank of Japan Report: Potential Increase in Medium to Long-Term Inflation Expectations
data.description: The Bank of Japan has released a report indicating that certain conditions may intensify pressure on medium to long-term inflation expectations. According to Jin10, the report highlights factors that could contribute to rising inflationary pressures over an extended period. The analysis suggests that ongoing economic developments and policy measures may play a significant role in shaping future inflation trends. The Bank of Japan continues to monitor these dynamics closely as they assess the potential impact on the country's economic outlook.

data.title: Japan's Core CPI May Hover Around 3% in Risk Scenario, BOJ Report Says
data.description: The Bank of Japan (BOJ) has released a report indicating that in a risk scenario, the core Consumer Price Index (CPI) could significantly exceed the BOJ's median baseline forecast. According to Jin10, the report suggests that the core CPI may hover around 3% during the fiscal years 2026 and 2027.

data.title: Federal Reserve Leadership Transition: Kevin Warsh's Potential Policy Directions
data.description: The article reviews the tenure of the current Federal Reserve leadership, focusing on their handling of the pandemic, inflation misjudgments, and aggressive interest rate hikes. According to PANews, it also looks ahead to the potential policy directions under the new chair, Kevin Warsh. The analysis includes discussions on the risks to the Federal Reserve's independence and the potential market impacts.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Tops List of Muted Topics on X Platform
data.description: X Platform's product head, Nikita Bier, announced that since the introduction of the 'mute' feature, cryptocurrency has become the most muted topic by users. According to Odaily, following cryptocurrency, politics and the Iran conflict are the next most muted subjects.

data.title: Binance Spot to List USDT/KZT Trading Pair and Launch Trading Bot Service
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to expand its trading options by introducing the USDT/KZT spot trading pair. Trading for this pair will commence on 2026-05-04 at 08:00 (UTC). Additionally, Binance will activate Trading Bots services for the USDT/KZT pair at the same time, enhancing the trading experience for users. The KZT is a fiat currency and does not represent any digital currencies. Binance facilitates KZT deposits and withdrawals via bank transfer with zero fees or through Mastercard.

Users interested in trading the new pair must complete account verification, and eligibility is determined by the user's country or region of residence. Currently, residents from Canada, Cuba, Crimea Region, Iran, Netherlands, North Korea, Syria, the United States and its territories, and non-government controlled areas of Ukraine are restricted from trading this pair. Binance reserves the right to disqualify users involved in dishonest behavior, such as wash trading or market manipulation, and those tampering with Binance program code. The list of restricted countries may change due to legal and regulatory updates. Users are encouraged to stay informed about any changes that may affect their trading eligibility.

data.title: Japan's March New Home Construction Declines Sharply
data.description: Japan's new home construction in March saw a significant decline, with the annual rate dropping by 29.29%. According to Jin10, this figure was worse than the anticipated decrease of 28.5% and followed a previous decline of 4.90%.

data.title: Forefront Tech Holdings Acquisition Corp Completes $100 Million IPO
data.description: Forefront Tech Holdings Acquisition Corp, a special purpose acquisition company, has announced the pricing of its initial public offering (IPO), raising a total of $100 million. According to Foresight News, the company is set to begin trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol FTHAU on April 30. The funds raised from the IPO will be used to explore merger and acquisition opportunities in the blockchain, artificial intelligence, fintech, and other emerging technology sectors.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI Founder Altman to Collaborate on Secure Internet Access
data.description: OpenAI founder Sam Altman has announced plans to collaborate with the entire ecosystem and government entities to explore secure methods of internet access. According to Jin10, Altman expressed a desire to swiftly assist in ensuring the safety of businesses and infrastructure.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI to Launch GPT-5.5-Cyber for Network Defense
data.description: OpenAI founder Sam Altman announced the upcoming release of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a cutting-edge cybersecurity model, aimed at key network defenders. According to Jin10, this model is set to be launched in the coming days, promising advancements in cybersecurity capabilities.

data.title: Elon Musk Highlights OpenAI's Original Mission in Trial Testimony
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter) that during the second day of the OpenAI trial, Elon Musk emphasized the organization's initial design as a nonprofit, open-source AI safety entity. The primary goal was to counterbalance Google and prevent AI from being monopolized by a few companies. Musk clarified that while he is not opposed to establishing for-profit entities, it is crucial that commercial interests do not overshadow the organization's original philanthropic mission. He also mentioned having previously warned about potential risks.

data.title: Hungary's Central Bank Governor Varga: Inflation May Exceed 5% in Second Half of 2026
data.description: Hungary's Central Bank Governor, Varga, has indicated that inflation could surpass 5% in the latter half of 2026. According to Jin10, this projection reflects concerns over potential economic pressures that may arise during this period. The statement underscores the need for careful monitoring of inflationary trends as the year progresses.

data.title: Vitalik made $355,000 selling free Meme tokens.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin (vitalik.eth) has been continuously selling the Meme tokens he acquired for free, seemingly emptying his wallet. In the past 24 hours, he earned 114,566 USDC and 155 ETH, totaling approximately $355,000, by selling these free Meme tokens.

data.title: Hungary's Central Bank Governor Varga: Forint Appreciation Supports Inflation Target
data.description: Hungary's Central Bank Governor Varga stated that the appreciation of the forint is beneficial for the central bank in achieving its inflation target. According to Jin10, Varga emphasized the positive impact of the stronger currency on the country's economic goals. The central bank continues to monitor currency movements closely as part of its broader strategy to manage inflation effectively.

data.title: Paxos, Solana, and other organizations have announced their support for the OKX Agent payment protocol.
data.description: According to official news, OKX Onchain OS released the Agent Payments Protocol (APP) yesterday. Subsequently, ecosystem participants from various fields including public chains, infrastructure, and AI expressed their support on the X platform. Currently, over twenty institutions have publicly announced their support, including Paxos, Solana, Aptos, Pharos Network, Nansen, Sui, 0G Labs, HeySorin AI, RootData, Corbits, QuickNode, Otto AI, Ethy AI, AltLayer, pieverse, Optimism, Surf AI, KITE AI, Mesh, Sahara AI, Teneo Protocol, Zerion, Alibaba Cloud, and Uniswap. The Agent Payments Protocol (APP) is an open payment protocol for AI Agent commercial activities, aiming to support agents in expanding their capabilities from payment to complete business processes.

data.title: Potential Volatility in Global Markets as VIX and Brent Crude Futures Diverge
data.description: Kryptanium Capital co-founder Daniel Yan highlighted on social media that there was a significant divergence between the VIX and Brent crude futures in April. According to ChainCatcher, this divergence is attributed to the resilience of the S&P 500 index. Yan described this situation as a 'fat tail risk,' noting that volatility shocks had already impacted global macro markets at the end of January and February. He suggested that a third shock could occur soon, and it would not be surprising.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Revises Interest Rate Forecast for 2027
data.description: Morgan Stanley has updated its projections regarding the Federal Reserve's interest rate policy. According to NS3.AI, the firm now anticipates that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates throughout 2026. Additionally, Morgan Stanley predicts two 25 basis point reductions in January and March 2027. This marks a shift from their earlier forecast, which expected rate cuts in September and December.

data.title: Jack Dorsey Supports Bitcoin Community Release of Documentary on Julian Assange
data.description:
Block co-founder Jack Dorsey announced at the 'Bitcoin 2026' conference his support for the documentary 'The Six Billion Dollar Man' to be distributed through Bitcoin community channels. According to Foresight News, the film, directed by Eugene Jarecki, focuses on the U.S. government's pursuit of WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange. Despite winning awards at the Cannes Film Festival and the Golden Globe Awards, mainstream streaming platforms have refused to release it. The Bitcoin community aligns closely with Assange's principles of information freedom.

In 2011, WikiLeaks faced payment channel blockades from Visa, MasterCard, and PayPal, with Bitcoin serving as the sole unblocked payment method, which Dorsey considers one of Bitcoin's most significant early use cases. He described this release as an experiment to 'bypass media gatekeepers,' with plans for global synchronized viewing parties using Bitcoin and the Nostr protocol. The film also includes an exclusive interview with Edward Snowden, revealing details of WikiLeaks' assistance in his escape from Hong Kong to Moscow.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Competition Intensifies for Enterprise-Level Smart Factory Solutions
data.description: The landscape of enterprise-level smart factory solutions is evolving as multiple models emerge in the market. According to PANews, the focus is shifting from relying on a single model to integrating various intelligent systems to enhance operational efficiency. Companies are exploring diverse approaches to leverage artificial intelligence in manufacturing processes, aiming to optimize production and reduce costs. As the industry progresses, the challenge lies in identifying the most effective combination of technologies to create a truly intelligent factory environment. This development marks a significant shift in how businesses approach automation and AI integration, emphasizing the need for adaptability and innovation in the manufacturing sector.

data.title: PayPal Announces Strategic Restructuring to Focus on Core Business Areas
data.description:
PayPal has announced a strategic restructuring, simplifying its operations into three core segments: Payments and Checkout with PayPal, Consumer Financial Services with Venmo, and Cryptocurrency and Payment Services. According to ChainCatcher, the Cryptocurrency and Payment Services segment has been designated as a core business unit, integrating payment processing, platform capabilities, and crypto operations, including Braintree, small merchant payment processing, value-added services, and the stablecoin PYUSD. Jeff Pomeroy has been appointed as the interim head of this segment.
Enrique Lores, PayPal's President and CEO, stated that the restructuring aims to accelerate the execution of long-term growth strategies, streamline decision-making processes, and foster innovation. The company plans to drive sustainable growth by aligning more closely with consumer needs, enhancing collaboration among the three business units, and improving operational efficiency. PayPal is expected to provide further details on its new operational model during its earnings call on May 5.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged in 2026.
data.description: On April 30th, Morgan Stanley: It expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged in 2026, down from its previous forecast of rate cuts in September and December. It now anticipates the Fed will cut rates by 25 basis points each in January and March 2027. (Jinshi)
